Empires & Puzzles Serandite Hero Review: The Pyromaniac Ninja
Is Serandite's burn hot enough to melt modern defenses, or has this Ninja's flame flickered out? We analyze her unique charge mechanics and massive healing reduction.
Serandite Review: The Arsonist Ninja Who Hates Healers
Serandite: The Pyromaniac Ninja Who Melts Healers (and Faces)

Serandite, the "Ninja of the Piercing Flames," burst onto the scene in early 2023 as part of the second wave of Ninja Tower heroes. As a 5-star Fire Barbarian, she introduced a terrifying combination of high-speed AoE damage and crippling healing reduction. While she doesn't have a costume yet, her base form remains a unique tactical nuke. Think of her as Gravemaker's angrier, more patient cousin who decided that simply burning enemies wasn't enough—they shouldn't be allowed to visit the hospital afterwards, either.

Serandite’s design screams "stealth but make it arson." Clad in deep crimson ninja garb with piercing eyes visible above a mask, she wields a bow that seems to be firing pure magma. The aesthetic fits her kit perfectly: sharp, dangerous, and likely to leave a mark. The community has had quite the debate over Serandite's gender—with "mineral deposit" being a surprisingly popular third option—but regardless of pronouns, the art effectively conveys a sense of fiery urgency.
Let’s be blunt: by modern 2026 standards, Serandite’s stats are showing their age. Her base attack of
was monstrous in 2023, but with newer heroes casually sporting

, she has fallen behind the power creep curve. Her defense (788) and health (1435) are particularly low, earning her the "Glass Cannon" title. Even with Limit Breaks, she remains squishy compared to the walking fortresses released in recent months. However, her high attack stat is still relevant because it directly scales her Burn damage, which is her bread and butter.
The Ninja Charge mechanic is Serandite's greatest asset and her biggest headache.

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Ninja family bonus is crucial for Serandite’s survival. A chance to
gives her a lifeline, considering her low defense. Pairing her with another Ninja (like the 4-star healers or another hitter) boosts these odds, making her deceptively slippery.

is exactly what the doctor ordered. Since she is naturally squishy, the Defense Up aether power helps mitigate her biggest weakness at the start of the battle, giving you time to find those crucial red tiles.

Upon release, Serandite was a top-tier threat. Her burn damage was so high in Beta it had to be nerfed, and even the "nerfed" version was capable of melting teams. She was a staple in defenses for her speed and punish potential.
Direct damage dealers age the fastest. Her 340% hit on x3 charge is now laughable compared to heroes doing 600%+ at Average speed. However,
DoT (Damage over Time) and Utility age slower
. Her burn is still potent, and her healing reduction is timelessly useful.
She is no longer a premier defense hero due to her low stats. However, on
, she remains a tactical weapon. She excels in breaking "Stall" defenses that rely on heavy healing and boosting. She is also a queen of the
and other events where you can charge her up safely.

Ninjas are often awkward in Rush, but her specific utility (anti-heal) is great against the inevitable Rush healers like Vivica or Kunchen.

Pair with other Barbarians or Burn heroes (like
Serandite + Anti-Healers (e.g., Perkin, GSD) + Blockers. The goal is to ensure the enemy health bar only goes down, never up.
Serandite + 2 other Ninjas (e.g., Ruby, Cobalt). Activates the high chance for Dodge/Counter, giving her much-needed survivability.
Rigard, Vivica, and modern cleansers wipe her threat instantly.
Garnet, Vanda, or any hero that grants immunity prevents her burn and heal debuff.

If you are lucky enough to pull her early, she is a game-changer. Focus on her Attack. Learn to count tiles—firing a x1 charge to stop a healer is often better than greedily waiting for a x2 charge and dying.
She is likely sitting on your bench more often these days. She isn't worth an Alpha Aether (LB2) purely for stats compared to a 2025 hero,
you lack a strong red anti-healer. However, if you have the "make ailments uncleansable" heroes, she becomes a top-tier combo piece again.
The community spent months arguing if Serandite was male, female, or a rock. The consensus settled on "she," but the ambiguity remains a meme.

When first leaked, her damage numbers were even higher. The community reaction was essentially, "Thank god they nerfed her, or we'd all be dead." Even post-nerf, she was considered a "Must Have" for months.
Players were initially confused why the card said one thing and the damage did another. It was Serandite who taught many players that DoT scales with Attack troops and Limit Breaks!

Serandite is like a classic muscle car: she might not have the safety features or the raw horsepower of the newest models, but she has style and a very specific, dangerous set of skills. While she may not be the terrifying monster she was in 2023, the ability to turn off enemy healing is a utility that never truly goes out of style.
A solid depth hero for war teams and a situational star, but no longer the main event.
N/A (No costume yet, but fingers crossed for a stat boost!)
